title: oligocladus patagonicus
intro: Oligocladus patagonicus, also known as the Patagonian club moss, is a type of ancient, non-vascular plant that belongs to the club moss family (Lycopodiaceae). It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting immune system function. Oligocladus patagonicus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine in South America.
